steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The balance is $905.40 at month 4, then grows by 0.1% every 2 months. We need the exponent to count the number of 2-month periods elapsed since month 4 — that is, $ rac{x-4}{2}$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$B(x)=905.40(1.001)^{2x-8}$': exponent $2x-8$ grows twice as fast as $x$, compounding every half-month rather than every 2 months. ✗
  • Choice B — '$B(x)=905.40(1.001)^{2x-4}$': exponent $2x-4$ still grows at rate $2$ per month — same error. ✗
  • Choice C — '$B(x)=905.40(1.001)^{ rac{x}{2}- rac{4}{2}}$': exponent $ rac{x}{2}-2= rac{x-4}{2}$ counts exactly the number of 2-month periods since month 4. ✓
  • Choice D — '$B(x)=905.40(1.001)^{ rac{x}{2}-4}$': exponent $ rac{x}{2}-4$ is off — at $x=4$ it gives $-2$ instead of $0$, so the anchor is wrong. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
